req.title: Run payroll
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-072 covers four distinct behavioural zones: (1) the happy-path where a valid payroll run completes and produces pay-stubs plus an audit entry (EP – valid partition); (2) a concurrent-run conflict that must return 409 (NEG – guard condition enforcing an error pathway); (3) atomicity on a forced internal failure – the run must roll back entirely; this is a negative test exercising the error pathway 'failed run shall not partially commit', so kind=negative and heuristic=NEG (not STC which is reserved for positive state-transition coverage); (4) a boundary run with exactly one active employee – the minimum non-empty employee set (BVA). A fifth case covers the state-transition arc idle→in-progress→completed to validate the full lifecycle (STC, positive). Together these five cases satisfy the minimum three required and give full coverage of the happy path, both error pathways, and the boundary condition stated in the requirement. The violation fix is: test[2] previously carried kind=negative with heuristic=STC; STC must only be used for positive state-transition traversal. The atomic-rollback test is a negative fault-injection scenario, so heuristic is corrected to NEG.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_completes_for_active_employees
preconditions: At least two active employees exist with known salary records
No payroll run is currently in-progress for the target period
actions: Poll or await until run state == completed
expected: POST returns 201 with run_id and state in [pending, in_progress, completed]
Final state of the run is completed
pay-stubs count equals active employee count
Each stub contains gross_pay, deductions, net_pay with net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
Audit log contains exactly one entry referencing run_id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_payroll_run_completes_for_active_employees(hr_admin_client, active_employees, payroll_period):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json={"period": payroll_period})
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["run_id"]

    completed = poll_until_state(hr_admin_client, run_id, target="completed", timeout=30)
    assert completed, f"Run {run_id} did not reach completed state"

    stubs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs")
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    stubs = stubs_resp.json()["pay_stubs"]
    assert len(stubs) == len(active_employees)

    for stub in stubs:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(
            stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"], rel=1e-4
        )

    audit_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/audit-log?ref={run_id}")
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json()["entries"]) >= 1

tests.name: test_concurrent_payroll_run_returns_409
preconditions: A payroll run for the same period is already in-progress (state=in_progress)
actions: Seed an in-progress run via fixture or direct DB helper
POST /api/payroll-runs with the same period payload
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict is returned
Response body contains an error code indicating run already in progress
No new run record is created
trace: R-WEB-072: 409 if another run is in progress
tests.executable:
def test_concurrent_payroll_run_returns_409(hr_admin_client, in_progress_payroll_run, payroll_period):
    # in_progress_payroll_run fixture seeds a run in state=in_progress for payroll_period
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json={"period": payroll_period})
    assert resp.status_code == 409

    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["in_progress", "conflict", "already running"])

    runs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs?period={payroll_period}")
    assert runs_resp.status_code == 200
    runs = runs_resp.json()["payroll_runs"]
    assert len(runs) == 1, "Duplicate run record must not be created"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_atomic_rollback_on_forced_failure
preconditions: Fault-injection is enabled so the payroll computation raises an error mid-run
No prior run exists for the target period
actions: Enable fault-injection header or toggle via test-control endpoint
Poll until run reaches a terminal state (failed or completed)
Inspect pay-stub records and run state
expected: Run reaches state=failed (not completed)
HTTP 500 is returned on the triggering request OR state transitions to failed
Zero pay-stub records exist for the run_id
Partial commit must not exist: no stub for any employee
trace: R-WEB-072: 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-072: failed run shall not partially commit
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_atomic_rollback_on_forced_failure(
    hr_admin_client, fault_injection_client, active_employees, payroll_period
):
    fault_injection_client.enable("payroll_computation_error")
    try:
        resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json={"period": payroll_period})
        assert resp.status_code in (500, 201, 202)

        run_id = None
        if resp.status_code in (201, 202):
            run_id = resp.json()["run_id"]
            poll_until_state(hr_admin_client, run_id, target="failed", timeout=30)
        else:
            run_id = resp.json().get("run_id")

        if run_id:
            stubs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs")
            stubs = stubs_resp.json().get("pay_stubs", [])
            assert len(stubs) == 0, "Partial commit detected after rolled-back run"

            state_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}")
            assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "failed"
    finally:
        fault_injection_client.disable("payroll_computation_error")

tests.name: test_payroll_run_state_transition_idle_to_completed
preconditions: At least one active employee exists
No payroll run is in-progress for the target period
actions: Verify no run exists for the period (idle state)
Poll state until in_progress is observed
Poll state until completed is observed
Confirm no further state changes occur
expected: Initial state query returns no active run (idle)
POST returns 201 and initial state is pending or in_progress
State transitions to in_progress before completed
Final state is completed and does not regress
The full arc idle -> in_progress -> completed is traversed
tests.executable:
import time

def test_payroll_run_state_transition_idle_to_completed(
    hr_admin_client, active_employees, payroll_period
):
    idle_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs?period={payroll_period}")
    assert idle_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(idle_resp.json().get("payroll_runs", [])) == 0, "Expected no run in idle state"

    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json={"period": payroll_period})
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["run_id"]
    initial_state = resp.json().get("state", "pending")
    assert initial_state in ("pending", "in_progress")

    observed_in_progress = initial_state == "in_progress"
    deadline = time.time() + 30
    while time.time() < deadline:
        state_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}")
        state = state_resp.json()["state"]
        if state == "in_progress":
            observed_in_progress = True
        if state == "completed":
            break
        time.sleep(0.5)

    final_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}")
    assert final_resp.json()["state"] == "completed"
    assert observed_in_progress, "State arc did not pass through in_progress"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_with_exactly_one_active_employee
preconditions: Exactly one active employee exists for the target period
No payroll run is in-progress for the period
actions: GET audit log for the run
expected: Run completes successfully with state=completed
Exactly one pay-stub record is created
Stub contains valid gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
Audit entry is present for the run
trace: R-WEB-072: pay-stub records created (boundary: single employee)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_payroll_run_with_exactly_one_active_employee(
    hr_admin_client, single_active_employee, payroll_period
):
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json={"period": payroll_period})
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["run_id"]

    completed = poll_until_state(hr_admin_client, run_id, target="completed", timeout=30)
    assert completed

    stubs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/payroll-runs/{run_id}/pay-stubs")
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    stubs = stubs_resp.json()["pay_stubs"]
    assert len(stubs) == 1

    stub = stubs[0]
    assert stub["employee_id"] == single_active_employee["id"]
    assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
    assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
    assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(
        stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"], rel=1e-4
    )

    audit_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/audit-log?ref={run_id}")
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json()["entries"]) >= 1
